title: A SAFE PLACE FOR NEWBORNS - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION A SAFE PLACE FOR NEWBORNS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon's "A Safe Place for Newborns" law allows a birth parent (mother or father) to leave a newborn infant at an authorized facility. This law was created so that a distressed parent may give up a baby safely, legally, and confidentially. A "Safe Place" to take a baby is a hospital, doctor's office, birthing clinic, police station, sheriff's office or fire department. A birth parent must give the baby to a person who works at the facility. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: As long as the baby is 60 days and older, is handed to someone working there, and shows no signs of abuse, criminal liability will be avoided. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: No identifying questions will be asked, however, it is helpful if the parent chooses to provide basic health information. The baby will be cared for and will get medical attention if needed. The Oregon Department of Human Services Child Welfare will have protective custody from the moment the infant is left at the facility. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Safe Havens for Abandoned Newborns Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ALERT IMMUNIZATION INFORMATION SYSTEM COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON SCREENWISE PROGRAM CONSUMER REGULATION - BIRTH CERTIFICATES CONSUMER REGULATION - OREGON VITAL RECORDS CONSUMER REGULATION - SENIOR FARM DIRECT APPLICATION FOR FARMERS & FARM STANDS DISASTER SERVICES - STATE EMERGENCY REGISTRY OF VOLUNTEERS IN OREGON DISASTER SERVICES - WINTER WEATHER ADVISORIES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - CLIMATE CHANGE AND HEALTH FOOD - SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM FOOD - WIC FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON IMMUNIZATION PROGRAM HUMAN REPRODUCTION - REPRODUCTIVE AND SEXUAL HEALTH HUMAN REPRODUCTION - STATE OF OREGON CONDOM DELIVERY PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - DEATH WITH DIGNITY ACT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PSILOCYBIN SERVICES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON PRESCRIPTION DRUG PROGRAM ARRAYRX PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON WIC ADMINISTRATION PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE INFORMATION PUBLIC HEALTH - ENVIRONMENTAL PUBLIC HEALTH PUBLIC HEALTH - LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH AUTHORITY DIRECTORY PUBLIC SAFETY - OHA DRINKING WATER DATA ONLINE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY HEARING DETECTION AND INTERVENTION SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES FOR CHILDREN SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - OREGON HOPELINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - TOBACCO QUIT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION
